The2018‘HousingandHealthGuidelines’bytheWorldHealthOrganization(WHO)emphasizedthat residentialfactorssuchasovercrowding,indoorairquality,andaccesstoessentialservicesarelinkedto buildingdesignandcanaffecthealth(WorldHealthOrganization2018).Modificationsinresidentialsettings mitigatetransmissionandpoorhealthoutcomes(Jatta2018).Potentialhousemodificationsforetal preventingmalariaina2022Cochranereviewcanbecharacterizedintoretrofitstrategiesandprimary constructioninterventions(Fox2019).Retrofitstrategiesincludescreening(Abong’o2022),etaletal replacingthatchedroofsorimprovingwalls(Ondiba2018),installingeavetubesorribbons(Kaindoaetal 2021,Sternberg2021),andimprovingenvelopedesigns(Ayanlade2019).Whiletraditionally,etaletaletal biomassfuelpollutionhasbeenassociatedwithmosquitoexit,recentfindingssuggestnoevidence supportingthenotionthatsmokefromdomesticbiomassfuelsoffersprotectionagainstmalaria(Biranetal 2007).Cookinginaseparatebuildingisfoundtoresultina26%reductioninmalarialinfectioncomparedto indoorcooking(Woolley2022).Recentarchitecturalinnovationshavefurtheradvancedmalariaetal preventionefforts(Mshamu2022,Chisumbe2024,ObeaguandObeagu2024,Cofone2025).etaletaletal The‘StarHomes’projectinTanzania,forinstance,featureselevatedbedroomsandpermeablescreened walls,resultinginsignificantreductionsinindoormosquitopopulationsandmalariatransmissionrisk comparedtotraditionalhousing(Mmbando2025).Similarly,studiesinMozambiquehaveetal demonstratedthatmodifiedhousingdesignscaneffectivelydecreaseindoormosquitodensities(Francisco andWatanabe2024).Yet,theseapproachesfrequentlyexhibitsynergisticorconflictingeffectsonmalaria transmissionandindooroverheating.Detailedbuildingdesignrecommendationsthatcanensure comprehensive‘good’healthinvulnerablecommunitieshavereceivedverylimitedattentioninresearch. Thereisanurgentneedforaffordable,scalable,andeffectivehousemodificationstrategiesthatcanbe implementedinthefaceofcomplexthreatsofoverheatingandenhancedvectordiseasepatterns.Underthis pretext,thisstudyaddressesthegapsbyexaminingthedualriskofheatstressandelevatedmalaria prevalenceinthirtyhouseholdsinKisumu,aKenyancityknownforitsnumeroussettlementsthatsuffer fromovercrowdingandlimitedresources(Abong’o2022).Amixed-methodapproachisadopted,etal couplingbuildingdesigndata,indoorclimaticmeteorologicaldata,andsocioeconomicdata,developinga holisticunderstandingofindoorhealthriskstoachieveoptimizedcontext-specifichousedesign interventions,cookingpractices,andoccupancybehavioraladaptations. Wedevelopedaconceptualframeworkforcoupledhealthriskassessmentbasedontheinfluencing factorsaroundstudyarea,localbuildingfeatures,andoccupants.Wealsoevaluatealow-costdesign intervention:replacingclosedroofeaveswithopeneavesfittedwithinsectscreens,aimedatenhancing ventilationwhilelimitingmosquitoentry.Theeffectivenessofthisinterventionisassessedthrough simulationmodeling.HeatandhealthchallengesinruralSub-SaharanAfricaposeacriticaldualthreatto vulnerablecommunities,yetcomprehensivedataremainsscarce(Anon2024).Ouropen-source, multi-dimensionalapproach—integratingdemographic,socioeconomic,climate,andbuildingcharacteristic data—willprovideavaluablecontributiontotheSub-SaharanAfricanclimate-healthscientificresearch.In 2 3Environ.Res.:Health(2025)035004HLietal methodssection,wepresentedthedataandmethodsemployedinthemixed-methodapproach,covering suchasfieldmeasurement,survey,computationalfluiddynamics(CFD)simulationanddetailedriskindex calculations.Resultssectionprovidesdetailedinsightsintomalariaandheatstressrisksobservedin investigatedhouses.Discussionsectiondiscussescriticalfindings,guidelinesforpreventingtherisks, limitations,andconclusions. 2.Methods 2.1.Fieldmeasurementandsurvey Tocapturetheoverlappingrisksofbothheatstressandmalariatransmission,ourfieldworkwasconducted duringtheearlytomid-Aprilperiod(11–14April2023).Thistimeframealignswiththeonsetofthelong rainyseason,whenambienttemperaturesremainhighandmalaria-transmittingmosquitoesareactive, makingitsuitableforassessingthecoupledhealththreats.Priortodatacollection,allparticipantswere providedwithdetailedinformationaboutthestudy’spurpose,procedures,potentialrisks,andbenefits. IPCCriskassessmentframeworkstatesthatvulnerabilityisunderstoodasafunctionofsensitivityand adaptivecapacity(Cardona2012).Indicatorsofsensitivityincludeelementslikeage,specificallytheetal proportionofchildrenandelderlywithintheoverallpopulation(Onyango2016).Occupantswithprioretal diseasesandchronicconditionsarealsoconsideredvulnerable(Campbell2018).Adaptivecapacityetal relatestothesocioeconomicstatusofapopulation,coveringfactorssuchaslowincome,unemployment,and financialstress,aswellasaccesstoresourcesforaddressingheat-relatedchallenges,suchashealthcare facilitiesandtheabilitytomovewithoutassistance(MullinsandWhite2020).Thedesignofhousesalso influencesoccupants’adaptivecapacity;theabsenceofopenings,coolingandventilationfacilities,and mosquitocontrolmeasuresareconsideredvulnerabilities(Samuelson2020).Theperceptionofheatisetal usuallylinkedtotheactiveimplementationofadaptivestrategies.Ifresidentsperceivethattemperaturesare increasing,theymaybemorelikelytotakeproactivemeasures,suchasimprovinginsulation,investinginair conditioning,orsupportingheatadaptationpolicies(Ban2019).Whentheperceptionmismatchestheetal actualchange,residentswillbeathighriskiftheriskisunderestimated. OursiteissituatedinthelakeendemicregionofwesternKenya,alongtheshoresofLakeVictoria.This regionhasthehighestmalariaprevalenceinthecountry(approximately27%)andexperiencesperennial transmissionwithseasonalpeaks,particularlyafterthelongrains(March–June)andshortrains (October–November).Themostcommonmosquitoesincludeand,An.arabiensis,An.funestusAn.gambiae andthemostcommonmalariaparasiteis(Atieli2009).ItishasbeenpredictedthattheP.falciparumetal risingtemperaturelargelyinfluencesthetransmissionofbyconsideringfactorssuchasparasiteP.falciparum establishmentandvectorcompetence(Caminade2014,Suh2024).Theheatstresspeaksjustbeforeetaletal orduringtheearlypartofthelongrainyseason(February–April),whenhightemperaturesandhumidity combine. BasedontheareasizeandnumberofhouseholdsinthevillageofKorandoB,Kisumu,Kenya,the residentpopulationisestimatedtobebetween1,500and3,000.Theblockclustersarespaced20–200meters apart,asdepictedinfigure1.Homeswererandomlyselectedfromeachblockclustertoensurepopulation 6 3Environ.Res.:Health(2025)035004HLietal Figure1.GeographicalcontextanddetailedsatelliteimageryofthestudyareainKenya.(a)Thelocationofthestudyareain Kenya,highlightedwitharedpinonthemapofAfrica,providesabroadergeographicalcontextwithinthecontinent.(b)Satellite mapofinvestigatedthirtytraditionalhousesinthevillageofKorandoB,Kisumu,Kenya.Eachcircleofareaconsistsof1–6 houses.(Basemap:Mapdata©2023Google). representation,withconsentobtainedfromallparticipatingresidents.Eachhomeisnumberedwithan anonymousnumber.138surveyedresidentsareallfrom30households.Thissamplesizeexceedsthe minimumthresholdof100respondentsandrepresentslessthan10%ofthetotalpopulation.Themarginof errorrangesfrom6%to8%,whichreferstotheexpectedrangeofsamplingerrorforproportionsestimated fromthesurveyresponses,calculatedusingstandardformulasforfinitepopulationcorrectionat95% confidencelevel(KrejcieandMorgan1996,MaschaandVetter2018). Theinvestigatedtraditionalhousesarecharacterizedbyarectangularshape,onelivingroom,andoneto 2twostrategicallyplacedbedrooms,andanareabelow10mforcooking.Homesareprimarilyconstructed usingrammedearthforthewallsandcorrugatedmetalroofs.Subsequently,thewallsandfloorsare plastered.Theroofsareconstructedwithatimberframe,rafters,andpurlins,supportingcorrugatediron sheets.Figure2(a)illustratestwoprevalentroofdesigns:hipandgable.Thehipdesign,preferredbylocal occupants,isconsideredmorestableandresilientagainstextremerainyweatherevents.Theroof incorporateseaveswitha200mm–400mminterval,providinganopenspacetotheenvironment.Thereare alsotwolayoutdesigns(figure2(b)),withasingledoororwithfrontandbackdoors. Inadditiontobuildingdesignfactors,wealsoconsideredenvironmentalelementsthatmayinfluence mosquitoabundance.Duringourfieldvisits,weobservedthatnoneofthe30householdshadaccesstopiped waterorrunningtaps.Instead,allreliedonmanuallystoredwaterforcooking,cleaning,andotherdomestic uses.Waterwastypicallystoredincoveredjerrycansorbuckets,oftenplacednearthecookingarea.These containersweregenerallywell-sealedanddidnotpresentvisibleconditionsconducivetomosquitobreeding.
